channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: The Trump administration’s warning last month that Europe must now take care of security in its own backyard raises troubling questions.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Naples struck by 4.4-magnitude quake causing minor damage and light injuries
item.description: ROME (AP) — The southern Italian city of Naples was struck by a 4.4-magnitude quake early Thursday that caused only minor damage and sent 11 people to the hospital, the most serious suffering contusions after part of a ceiling collapsed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guatemala steps up patrols along border as US extends border security goals south
item.description: OCOS, Guatemala (AP) — As the United States government works to effectively extend its border security objectives south into Central America, countries like Guatemala have come under renewed scrutiny and pressure to step up their own border enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin will always be a threat to stability in Europe: Dissident
item.description: Ukrainian leaders have already agreed to a 30-day halt in fighting, and now, U.S. diplomats are seeking Russia’s acceptance.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico’s second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: Diana De La Mora And Fabiola Sánchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kuwait frees 10 jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released 10 American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies. “President Trump has made it a priority to bring home Americans detained overseas.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans think Trump is too close to Russia
item.description: A majority of Americans think President Trump is too closely aligned with Russia, including more than a quarter of Republicans, a poll released Thursday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: UN official denies seeing 'a shred of evidence' showing staff in Gaza held hostages
item.description: Tom Fletcher, a top UN humanitarian aid official, says he has not seen "a shred of evidence" linking the international body's workers in Gaza to Hamas hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN: Sudan war is world's worst humanitarian crisis: 30 million need aid, 16 million of them kids
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The war in Sudan has created the world’s largest and most devastating humanitarian crisis with over 30 million people needing aid this year, 16 million of them children trying to survive in dire conditions, the head of the U.N. children’s agency said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin thanks Trump for principles of ceasefire push, but does not say yes
item.description: Russian President Putin says he agrees in "principle" with President Trump's desire for a 30-day ceasefire but argued it would only help Ukraine and said he wants a lasting peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu slams 'terrorist-supporting' UN council that accused Israel of committing sexual crimes
item.description: Israeli and American officials are condemning a report from the United Nations Human Rights Council that accuses Israel of carrying out systemic acts of sexual violence against Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijacking and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister on Thursday commended the country’s armed forces for successfully rescuing 339 passengers after a deadly train hijacking by insurgents in the southwest. A total of 25 people were killed by the attackers.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration empties Guantánamo of migrants, flies all back to US
item.description: The Trump administration’s plans to detain migrants in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, hit another roadblock this week after all 40 such individuals held there were sent back to the United States, with no future deportation flights scheduled to the island.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian leader signs constitution that puts the country under an Islamist group's rule for 5 years
item.description: Syria’s interim president has signed a temporary constitution that leaves the country under Islamist rule for five years during a transitional phase.
item.dc:creator: Ghaith Alsayed And Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Duterte refused fingerprinting and threatened lawsuits during chaotic arrest, Philippine police say
item.description: A Philippine general says that ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte threatened him with lawsuits, refused to be fingerprinted and told law enforcers “you have to kill me to bring me to The Hague.”.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethiopia's Tigray regional government appeals for help after factional fighters seize key towns
item.description: ADDIS ABABA, Ethiopia (AP) — The interim government of Ethiopia’s Tigray region appealed for the Ethiopian federal government to intervene after a faction of the Tigray People's Liberation Front seized control of two major towns, leaving several people wounded and raising fears of a return to civil
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL GETACHEW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Top diplomats from G7 countries meet in Canada as Trump threatens more tariffs on US allies
item.description: Top diplomats from Group of 7 have held a first day of talks in Canada as U.S. President Donald Trump's trade and foreign policies have thrown the bloc's once solid unity into disarray.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: They crossed the world to reach the US. Now deported under Trump, they're stuck in Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — They crossed oceans to get to the U.S., fleeing conflict, religious persecution, poverty and government crackdowns in countries such as Afghanistan, Somalia, Cameroon, China, Pakistan and Iran.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Emirati diplomat identified as having a letter from Trump meets with Iran's foreign minister
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An Emirati diplomat earlier identified by Tehran as carrying a letter from U.S. President Donald Trump seeking to jump-start talks over Iran's rapidly advancing nuclear program met Wednesday with Iran's foreign minister in the Iranian capital.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo and M23 rebel group will hold peace talks on Tuesday, mediator Angola says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — The government of Congo will hold peace talks next week in Angola with the Rwanda-backed M23 rebel group that has captured key areas of Congo’s mineral-rich east, mediator Angola announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump envoys begin Russia outreach after Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: Trump administration officials are reaching out to their Russian counterparts as the White House says the ball is in Moscow’s court to agree to a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detained captain of cargo ship that collided with US oil tanker is Russian national, ship owner says
item.description: A Russian national was revealed to be the captain of the cargo ship that collided with an American tanker off the coast of England, spilling U.S. military jet fuel into the sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia waiting for US-Ukraine cease-fire proposal details, Kremlin says
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters Wednesday that Russia must get the details on U.S.-Ukrainian cease-fire talks before commenting on the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine, US say country open to 30-day ceasefire in war with Russia
item.description: Ukraine says in a joint statement with the United States that it is open to a 30-day ceasefire in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rubio says mineral deal ‘not main topic on agenda’ in Ukraine meeting
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio and a U.S. delegation will meet with Ukrainian leaders on Tuesday in Saudi Arabia to discuss ways to end the war between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump considering pressure on Russia and seeking deal with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said he’s “strongly considering” sanctions and tariffs on Russia in hopes of forcing a settlement to the war in Ukraine. Trump also sent a letter to Iran’s leaders seeking to negotiate a nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9
